title: Track Document and Report Incidents to Designated Officials and Authorities
description: track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization

checks.name: Incidents are Tracked
checks.purpose: Incident tracking via ticketing systems or incident logs is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Incidents are Documented
checks.purpose: Incident documentation including timelines, affected systems, and response actions is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Authorities to Whom Incidents are to be Reported are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of external reporting authorities such as US-CERT and DIBNet is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in the incident response plan and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Organizational Officials to Whom Incidents are to be Reported are Identified
checks.purpose: Identification of internal reporting officials such as ISSO and senior leadership is an organizational GRC documentation concern documented in the incident response plan and is not technically verifiable via automated check

checks.name: Identified Authorities are Notified of Incidents
checks.purpose: Notification of external authorities is an organizational process concern executed during incident response and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Identified Organizational Officials are Notified of Incidents
checks.purpose: Notification of internal organizational officials is an organizational process concern executed during incident response and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

remediation: Establish and document incident tracking and reporting procedures identifying both internal officials and external authorities such as US-CERT and DIBNet. Ensure reporting timelines comply with applicable CUI and DFARS requirements.
